genre_refs: law-enforcement 'End of Watch' / Wall of Honor memorial
description: A designed memorial 'Wall of Honor' tribute graphic in landscape format. The background is a dark, moody blue slate/stone texture; on the right side is a photograph of a U.S. flag and a bouquet of deep-red roses, lending a somber commemorative tone. Upper left, large white serif type reads 'Wall of Honor' next to a small ICE memorial pin/ribbon emblem (a blue ribbon with the ICE/DHS shield). Below the title, white text identifies the fallen officer: 'Special Agent in Charge / Roger L. Von Amelunxen', then a thin gold divider, the phrase 'End of Watch' (serif, with 'of' italic), and the date 'Wednesday, July 30, 1980.' A prominent inset photo at center-right shows an antique gold law-enforcement badge reading 'U.S. CUSTOMS / THE DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY' with a Treasury seal, '1789', and a banner 'PATROL OFFICER' and the number '271'. This is a respectful in-memoriam/legacy post honoring a Customs officer killed in the line of duty in 1980; the technique builds institutional sympathy, heritage, and esprit de corps. Target audience: ICE personnel, law-enforcement community, and the general public.
notable_text: Wall of Honor; Special Agent in Charge Roger L. Von Amelunxen; End of Watch; Wednesday, July 30, 1980; U.S. CUSTOMS THE DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY; PATROL OFFICER
